text.title: Segment-Aware Design System Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Segment-Aware Design System application must enforce. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Four states per gated feature: locked (showing the value proposition), preview (limited functional access), full (capability), and overflow (paid threshold hit).
rule.rationale: Without taxonomy, designers improvise; explicit four-state model creates consistency.

rule.statement: Extend tokens for segment-specific surfaces (e.g., `--accent-pro`, `--surface-locked`). Avoid hard-coded color/copy per surface.
rule.rationale: Hard-coded segment treatment fragments visual identity; token extensions keep system unified.

rule.statement: Upgrade CTAs appear contextually (at the moment of need), not as constant nags. Frequency cap: ≤2 per session.
rule.rationale: Constant upsell drives churn + ad-blocking; contextual upsell converts.

rule.statement: Locked state shows value, not friction. 'You're missing out' framing → 'Unlock X to do Y'. Patterns avoided: confirmshaming, hidden fees, fake scarcity.
rule.rationale: Dark patterns trigger consumer-protection actions + brand damage; ethical framing is sustainable.

rule.statement: Trials end gracefully (clear date + reminder); grandfathered users retain access on tier changes.
rule.rationale: Surprise downgrades = churn + bad reviews; explicit policy preserves trust.
